changeSet.author: nexus-jxizy.2
changeSet.comment: search_metadata_scoped_{d}: identical signature, body projects encode(chash,'hex') — hex is interchange (RDR-180 Item1); joins stay native bytea

changeSet.author: nexus-jxizy.2
changeSet.comment: search_graph_hop_{d}: matched-chunk chash output becomes encode(chash,'hex')

changeSet.author: nexus-jxizy.2
changeSet.comment: search_topic_scoped_{d}: chunk-level id output becomes encode(chash,'hex'); the topic_assignments join goes through encode() — doc_id STAYS TEXT (mixed identity space: chunk chashes AND memory-note titles; see rdr180-001 header)

changeSet.author: nexus-jxizy.2
changeSet.comment: manifest_orphans: RETURNS TABLE keeps chash text via encode(chash,'hex') at the projection

changeSet.author: nexus-jxizy.2
changeSet.comment: remap_membership: facts are hex TEXT, live keys are bytea — decode at the seam, and chain through chash_alias so era facts stay resolvable against a rekeyed store
changeSet.sql: CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION nexus.remap_membership( p_source_collection text, p_target_collection text ) RETURNS TABLE(mapped_total bigint, present_count bigint) LANGUAGE sql STABLE SECURITY INVOKER AS $fn$ WITH fact AS ( SELECT CASE WHEN r.new_chash ~ '^[0-9a-f]+$' AND length(r.new_chash) % 2 = 0 THEN decode(r.new_chash, 'hex') ELSE convert_to(r.new_chash, 'UTF8') END AS key_bytes FROM nexus.chash_remap r WHERE r.source_collection = p_source_collection AND r.target_collection = p_target_collection ), resolved AS ( SELECT COALESCE(a.new_chash, f.key_bytes) AS chash FROM fact f LEFT JOIN nexus.chash_alias a ON a.old_bytes = f.key_bytes ) SELECT count(*)::bigint AS mapped_total, count(*) FILTER (WHERE EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM nexus.chunks_384 c WHERE c.collection = p_target_collection AND c.chash = resolved.chash) OR EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM nexus.chunks_768 c WHERE c.collection = p_target_collection AND c.chash = resolved.chash) OR EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM nexus.chunks_1024 c WHERE c.collection = p_target_collection AND c.chash = resolved.chash) )::bigint AS present_count FROM resolved $fn$;
